title: Your Rights and the Grand Jury
publisher: Coalition to End Grand Jury Abuse
description: Pamphlet covering grand juries, fighting subpoenas, preparing for the grand jury, refusing to testify, and mounting an offensive campaign.

title: The FBI Story — Woodstock Times No. 41 (October 14, 1976)
description: Reproduction of an article on the FBI from the October 14, 1976 issue of the Woodstock Times.

title: If an Agent Knocks — Federal Investigators and Your Rights (March 1985)
description: March 1985 Center for Constitutional Rights pamphlet with frequently asked questions about the FBI and your rights when approached by federal investigators.

title: Chicago Guild Update — National Lawyers Guild (February 1985)
description: NLG Chicago Chapter newsletter (Feb 1985) covering highlights from the 1985 National Lawyers Guild Conference in Atlanta. (Note: cataloged filename references 'criminal justice' but the document is the Chicago Guild Update — Freedom Archives source data has crossed URLs for two records in this collection.)

title: Organizational Rights — Tips on Surveillance and Security
publisher: Minnesota Coalition to Prevent Repressive Federal Legislation
description: Factsheet on counteracting government surveillance of progressive organizations.

title: Common Sense Security — Tip Sheet for Staff Organizers (O'Donnel)
publisher: National Lawyers Guild Civil Liberties Committee; Political Repression Project of the Center for Constitutional Rights
description: Practical suggestions for resisting repressive techniques of the state, corporations, and the right wing, including how to respond to FBI visits.

title: Repression in the Land of the Free
publisher: International Network Against New State Repression
description: Documents exposing and resisting U.S. government attacks on domestic and international progressive and revolutionary movements.

title: End Grand Jury Repression — Committee Leaflet
publisher: Committee to End Grand Jury Repression
description: Single-leaflet flyer outlining the Committee to Stop Grand Jury Repression, the historical roots and current uses of grand juries, and how to organize against them.

title: Free to Protest — Dissent, Prophetic Ministry and the Bill of Rights (August 1985)
description: Pamphlet covering the history of political repression, new repression, legislative/executive acts, grand juries, prosecutors and agents, the Bill of Rights, and FBI visits.

title: Police Repression — N.Y. Committee Against Repression (1978)
description: Monograph on police murders and police/FBI alliances against oppressed communities, framing repression as the U.S. government's answer to the just struggle of oppressed people.

title: The New State Repression — Ken Lawrence (1985)
publisher: International Network Against New State Repression
description: Analyzes political repression in three forms: police brutality, vigilantism, and secret police. Covers the strategy of permanent repression, Frank Kitson's counterinsurgency theory, and Louis Giuffrida under Reagan.

title: Letter to 'Friends' — Committee to End Grand Jury Repression
publisher: Committee to End Grand Jury Repression
description: Correspondence informing Bay Area progressive groups of developments in the Special Federal Grand Jury convened to investigate the SLA.

title: Firmes En La No Colaboración (1983)
description: Spanish-language monograph on the politics of non-collaboration with grand juries, with sections on Albizu Campos and the grand jury.

title: Comité de California Contra La Represión
publisher: Comité de California Contra La Represión
description: Pamphlet with a short history of the Comité de California Contra la Represión and its principles of unity under the slogan 'Repression Breeds Resistance.'

title: Our Constitutional Rights — Statement to SF Grand Jury (Clayton Van Lydegraf, 1973)
description: Reproduction of Clayton Van Lydegraf's statement before a San Francisco grand jury in January-February 1973.

title: A Guide to the Grand Jury — Kathy Boudin & Brian Glick (Liberation, June 1969)
description: June 1969 Liberation magazine article on how the grand jury is used to dismantle political movements.

title: The Political Grand Jury — An Instrument of Repression
publisher: New York Committee Against Grand Jury Repression
description: Pamphlet with background information on how a grand jury works, the political grand jury, non-collaboration, and what readers can do.

title: Grand Juries — Did You Know? (Puerto Rican Independence)
description: Pamphlet on how the Puerto Rican independence movement was being targeted by grand juries.

title: The FBI Is in Town — Don't Talk (National Lawyers Guild)
description: Pamphlet informing people of their right not to talk to the FBI: tricks to watch for, how to refuse, and what to do if agents visit friends.

title: Emergency Committee to Stop the Grand Jury (September 25, 1989)
publisher: Committee to Stop the Grand Jury
description: Press release, a National Conference of Black Lawyers letter, JBAKC attorneys' Chicago court papers, and subpoenaed public documents of the John Brown Anti-Klan Committee.

title: Grand Jury and FBI Activities
description: Reproduction of a report on nationwide grand juries and their activities.

title: Berkeley Tribe Vol. 6 No. 5 (1971) — Grand Jury Coverage
description: Issue of Berkeley Tribe (Vol. 6, No. 5, 1971) containing a Tom Miller article on grand juries.

title: Statement of Shelley Miller to the Grand Jury (February 2, 1983)
description: Shelley Miller's statement refusing to testify before a grand jury, framed as a defense of the Puerto Rican nation's right to fight U.S. imperialism.

title: National Peoples Moratorium 1979 — Against Police, INS, BIA Crimes and Grand Jury / FBI Repression
description: Bilingual (English/Spanish) 1979 flyer for a conference to discuss and organize against government repression of social movements.

title: Non-Collaboration — The Only Answer to Political Investigations (1985)
authors: Bob Lederer; Sandra Roland; Steven Burke; Julie Nalibov; Christine Rico
description: Statements from JBAKC-connected grand-jury resisters refusing to cooperate with the federal probe into the 1983 Capitol / War College / Navy Officers Club / Navy computer bombings claimed by the Armed Resistance Unit and Red Guerrilla Resistance. Includes a Terry Bisson poem.

title: Repression Breeds Resistance — Stop the RICO Grand Jury (Benefit Forum Flyer)
description: Flyer for a Benefit/Forum/Dinner against political repression of revolutionary forces through the use of grand juries. Speakers include Kwame Kalimara, Jose Lopez, Silvia Baraldini, and Alan Berkman.
